blog.title: Dependabot Cooldown: Configure the New 3-Day Default

long.title: Which Tasks Should You Delegate to an AI Agent?

results.content:
Dependabot's 3-day cooldown does not delay security fixes. Use this indie-repo YAML and merge gate.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/dependabot-default-cooldown-indie-repo-checklist/?utm_source=x&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-17&utm_content=blog

Dependabot version update PRs now wait three days by default, while security updates still open immediately. This guide turns that change into a practical indie-repo policy: risk-tiered YAML examples, narrow exceptions, and a five-step merge gate. Use it before changing `.github/dependabot.yml`.

Dependabot version updates now wait three days by default, but security updates still open immediately. The tricky part is turning that delay into a safer review process instead of a slower inbox. This guide includes practical YAML starting points and a repeatable merge gate.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/dependabot-default-cooldown-indie-repo-checklist/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-17&utm_content=blog

Dependabot now adds a three-day buffer to version updates, without delaying security fixes. This guide gives indie repos a practical YAML policy and evidence-based merge gate.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/dependabot-default-cooldown-indie-repo-checklist/?utm_source=mastodon&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-17&utm_content=blog #DevSecOps #GitHub
results.repair: Removed the thumbnail through the Postiz update API after local SSRF protection blocked the media fetch, then retried the existing post through Temporal.

results.content:
Dependabot's new 3-day cooldown is useful only if your merge gate is stronger than “the release is old enough.” Here’s the YAML policy and review checklist.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/dependabot-default-cooldown-indie-repo-checklist/?utm_source=nostr&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-17&utm_content=blog

Parallelization isn’t just “go faster.” It can split independent work—or compare multiple attempts. This 63-second Tiny Agent shows when each pattern helps.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MP5fvoVjwtI #AIAgents

Parallelization has two different jobs: split independent work to reduce elapsed time, or run multiple attempts to compare results and improve confidence. This 63-second Tiny Agent gives a simple rule for choosing the right pattern—and avoiding parallel work that only adds coordination cost.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MP5fvoVjwtI

“Run it in parallel” sounds like a speed trick, but that is only half the story. Parallel attempts can also expose disagreement and improve confidence. This 63-second Tiny Agent shows when to split the work and when to compare the answers.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MP5fvoVjwtI

Parallelization can reduce time—or improve confidence by comparing multiple attempts. This 63-second Tiny Agent shows when each pattern earns its coordination cost.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MP5fvoVjwtI #AIAgents #Automation

Parallel agents have two different jobs: divide independent work, or compare multiple attempts. This 63-second Tiny Agent explains when parallelism improves speed—and when it improves confidence.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MP5fvoVjwtI

A longer prompt doesn’t make a task agent-ready. Use this Task Upgrade Scorecard to decide when AI should answer, assist, work under supervision, or run a reusable workflow.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SJ0g9yxI3_o #AIAgents

The shift from chat to delegated work should start with the task, not the model. This Task Upgrade Scorecard tests for concrete output, accessible context, observable feedback, and bounded risk—then shows when to keep a human in the loop and when to package the work as a reusable agent workflow.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SJ0g9yxI3_o

Some tasks need a better prompt. Others need an agent with tools, context, feedback, and clear stopping conditions. This video gives you a practical scorecard for telling the difference before you over-automate the work.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SJ0g9yxI3_o

A longer prompt is not automatically an agent-ready task. Use this Task Upgrade Scorecard to test output, context, feedback, and risk before you delegate the workflow.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SJ0g9yxI3_o #AIAgents #AIWorkflow

Before turning a task into an agent workflow, check four things: concrete output, accessible context, observable feedback, and bounded risk. This video turns that test into a reusable scorecard.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SJ0g9yxI3_o
